channel.title: Disclosure on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Disclosure on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Pentagon Releases Batch of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files
item.description:
The Pentagon has released approximately 160 declassified documents concerning unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP), including never-before-seen footage and historical materials dating back to the 1940s. These files contain reports of various UAP sightings, such as “orbs launching orbs” and a “misshapen and uneven ball of white light,” as well as accounts from Apollo crews. The release aims to provide public transparency, though the government has not claimed to have detected extraterrestrial life. Public opinion suggests a belief that the government may be concealing information about UAP, with some thinking they are alien in origin.

Source: https://www.wired.com/story/pentagon-drops-new-ufo-files

item.title: Pentagon Releases Files Related to Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP)
item.description:
The Pentagon has begun releasing government files concerning unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP), also known as UFOs. These files include FBI reports on unexplained sightings and NASA debriefs with astronauts who observed unidentified objects on the moon. Images released alongside the files depict various phenomena, including an object seen by a U.S. military operator over the Middle East in May 2022 and a football-shaped UAP reported near Japan in 2024. Archival photographs from the Apollo 12 and Apollo 17 missions also show unidentified phenomena on the lunar surface.

Source: https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/see-the-pentagons-new-ufo-image-release

item.title: NASA Documents Show Renewed Internal Planning on How to Announce Discovery of Extraterrestrial Life
item.description:
NASA has released documents detailing internal discussions about how the agency would communicate a confirmed discovery of extraterrestrial life. These documents include a June 2025 meeting invitation focused on developing a formal communications protocol. This planning builds upon earlier work, with a 2020 presentation outlining foundational considerations for such a scenario, emphasizing equitable information dissemination and managing public reaction. While the documents confirm ongoing planning, they do not indicate any discovery has occurred, and significant portions of the deliberative process remain redacted.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/nasa-documents-show-renewed-internal-planning-on-how-to-announce-discovery-of-extraterrestrial-life

item.title: Linda Moulton Howe
item.description:
Linda Moulton Howe is an American investigative journalist and documentary filmmaker who rose to prominence for her reporting on environmental issues and, later, for her work in the field of ufology. She gained significant attention in 1980 for producing the documentary A Strange Harvest, which explored cattle mutilation theories and alleged extraterrestrial involvement in government activities. Throughout her career, Howe has contributed to numerous television programs and media projects related to UFOs, crop circles, and alien abductions, while frequently appearing as a guest on platforms like Coast to Coast AM. While some supporters regard her as a leading figure in the investigation of unexplained phenomena, critics such as Joe Nickell have characterized her work as credulous and lacking in verified evidence.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Moulton_Howe

item.title: New Emails Reveal Pentagon Effort to Align Messaging on AATIP and Luis Elizondo
item.description:
Newly released Pentagon emails from May 2019 reveal an internal contradiction regarding Luis Elizondo’s role in the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP). A senior Pentagon official, Neill Tipton, stated in an email that Elizondo had no assigned responsibilities related to AATIP during his time under Tipton. However, other emails show Tipton was directly communicating with Elizondo about AATIP and reviewing a memo concerning responsibilities for it. Pentagon officials expressed concern about these contradictions and the need for consistent messaging. Despite Tipton’s 2019 statement, previously released records show him engaging in direct discussions with Elizondo about AATIP responsibilities in 2017. Luis Elizondo disputes Tipton’s characterization, stating that documentary evidence shows Tipton was aware of his involvement and agreed to assume his responsibilities.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/new-emails-reveal-pentagon-effort-to-align-messaging-on-aatip-and-luis-elizondo

item.title: Newly Released Pentagon Records Center on UAP and Internal Email Communications
item.description:
A collection of Department of Defense records has been released, focusing on internal email communications involving Pentagon spokesperson Christopher Sherwood and references to Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP). These documents stem from a Freedom of Information Act request seeking insight into how UAP-related discussions occurred within the Pentagon’s public affairs office. The request specifically targeted emails sent to or from Sherwood containing keywords like “UAP.” While 416 pages were found responsive, portions were withheld under FOIA exemptions related to intelligence information and internal deliberations. The Black Vault has filed an appeal regarding the extensive redactions.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/pentagon-spokesperson-christopher-sherwood-emails-on-ufos-uap-and-more

item.title: Secret Space Program and 20 and Back: Fictional Stories as Fact
item.description:
The “secret space program” and “20 and back” are fictional narratives presented as fact within ufology. These stories describe individuals recruited into secret military programs for service on other planets, followed by age regression or time travel to return to their original time. The origins of these stories are linked to a 1970s British spoof documentary called “Alternative 3,” which evolved into a widespread myth. Corey Goode popularized these concepts, particularly on the Gaia program “Cosmic Disclosures,” claiming he was age-regressed after serving as a warrior with alien species. The terms “Secret Space Program” and “20 and Back” are trademarks owned by Goode.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secret_space_program

item.title: The Seekers: A UFO Religion in Mid-Twentieth Century United States
item.description:
The Seekers, also known as The Brotherhood of the Seven Rays, was a UFO religion founded in 1953 by Charles Laughead in the Midwestern United States. Led by Dorothy Martin, the group believed flying saucers would save them from a predicted catastrophe on December 21, 1954. When the prophecy failed, many members experienced cognitive dissonance, a phenomenon studied by Leon Festinger in his book When Prophecy Fails. The Seekers are considered by some to be the earliest UFO religion.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Seekers_%28rapturists%29

item.title: Archive of Luis Elizondo’s ‘Deleted’ Emails
item.description:
Luis Elizondo is a central figure in discussions about Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP) due to his alleged involvement with the Pentagon’s Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP). The Black Vault reported that the Department of Defense (DoD) destroyed Elizondo’s emails, hindering verification of his claims. To circumvent this, The Black Vault pursued FOIA requests for emails of individuals who likely corresponded with Elizondo, indirectly recovering some exchanges. This archive contains these recovered emails, as well as records where “no records” were found, offering insight into Elizondo’s interactions within the DoD.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/archive-of-luis-elizondos-deleted-emails

item.title: FOIA Request on UAP Keywords Produces Minimal Results, Heavy Redactions, from Energy Department
item.description:
A Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the U.S. Department of Energy for communications regarding unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP) yielded only two partially released documents. These documents, internal DOE emails from May 2024, were prepared for Secretary Jennifer Granholm in advance of an oversight hearing. The emails reference attached briefing materials that included “UFO and UPA talking points” for congressional engagement. Despite the request’s broad scope, significant portions of the released documents were withheld under FOIA exemptions, preventing the disclosure of substantive content or internal analysis on UAP topics.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/foia-request-on-uap-keywords-produces-minimal-results-heavy-redactions-from-energy-department

item.title: Project Sign report
item.description:
Project Sign, also known as Project Saucer, was a U.S. Air Force study of unidentified flying objects active for most of 1948. Established to collect and evaluate UFO sighting information due to potential national security concerns, it was the precursor to Project Grudge. A public paper released in 1949 stated that while some UFOs appeared to be actual aircraft, their origins could not be determined due to insufficient data. The project investigated claims related to inventor Jonathan Edward Caldwell’s disk-rotor aircraft, but found they could not explain the widespread “flying saucer” reports.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_Sign

item.title: Despite Trump’s Call to Release UAP Files, Navy Denies Appeal for 78 Classified UAP Photographs
item.description:
The U.S. Navy has formally denied an appeal seeking the release of 78 photographs designated as “unidentified aerial phenomena” (UAP). This decision upholds a previous denial of a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request filed by The Black Vault. The Navy cited classification under Executive Order 13526 and the UAP Classification Guide as the reason for withholding the images. Despite public calls for transparency and a former president’s stated desire to release such files, the Navy’s appeal authority deferred to the Original Classification Authority, stating that no portion of the photographs could be released.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/despite-trumps-call-to-release-uap-files-navy-denies-appeal-for-78-classified-uap-photographs

item.title: Closed-Door 2024 UAP Briefing: FOIA Discloses Select Media Invitees
item.description:
Newly released records obtained through a Freedom of Information Act request reveal journalists who were privately invited to an embargoed Pentagon media roundtable on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP). This event, held on March 6, 2024, was connected to the Department of Defense’s first volume of the congressionally mandated Historical Record Report on U.S. government UAP programs. The invitation, sent by Pentagon spokesperson Susan Gough, outlined strict conditions for participation, including agreeing to an embargo until the report’s public release on March 8. Journalists from outlets such as The New York Times, CNN, Politico, Task & Purpose, and The Washington Post were among those invited, though some recipients remain redacted due to privacy concerns.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/closed-door-2024-uap-briefing-foia-discloses-select-media-invitees

item.title: Yankee Blue: Part of Pentagon UFO Disinformation Campaign
item.description:
Yankee Blue was a fake United States Air Force program that purported to involve the reverse-engineering of alien spacecraft. In reality, it was an internal hazing and misinformation campaign, possibly part of a broader Pentagon disinformation program. For decades, personnel in classified Air Force units were shown “UFO” photos and told they were assisting in a top-secret alien-technology recovery program. This ritual persisted from the Cold War era until its discontinuation in 2023. The strategy likely originated as a security measure to protect sensitive programs and divert attention from advanced weapons development.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yankee_Blue

item.title: Six and a Half Years Later, the DoD’s Reply to Harry Reid’s AATIP Memo Remains Missing
item.description:
In June 2009, Senator Harry Reid requested heightened protection for the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP) in a letter to the Department of Defense. Despite the Department of Defense acknowledging the authenticity of this letter and stating it had responded, a formal response has not been publicly released. This absence of a documented reply conflicts with Pentagon statements and established procedures for handling congressional correspondence. The ongoing dispute over the existence and nature of AATIP, coupled with the difficulty in locating related documents, contributes to the mystery surrounding the program.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/six-and-a-half-years-later-the-dods-reply-to-harry-reids-aatip-memo-remains-missing

item.title: Pentagon Reopens Search for Alleged ‘Yankee Blue’ Memo After Initial Denial
item.description:
A FOIA appellate decision has ordered the Department of War to conduct additional searches for a 2023 memorandum. This memorandum was reportedly issued by the Secretary of Defense’s office to halt an Air Force hazing ritual called “Yankee Blue,” which involved fabricated claims of reverse-engineering non-human technology. Initially, the Department of War stated no such memorandum was found, but the appeal has now been granted, requiring a new search. The original Wall Street Journal article described the alleged directive but did not publish the memo or provide specific sourcing.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/pentagon-reopens-search-for-alleged-yankee-blue-memo-after-initial-denial

item.title: Citizens Against UFO Secrecy
item.description:
Citizens Against UFO Secrecy (CAUS) was a non-profit organization founded in 1977 to advocate for the release of classified information regarding UFOs. The group used the Freedom of Information Act to challenge government secrecy surrounding UFO contact. Peter Gersten, a former director, filed lawsuits against the CIA and NSA, resulting in the release of some UFO documents. CAUS is now listed as an inactive organization.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Citizens_Against_UFO_Secrecy

item.title: Fund for UFO Research (FUFOR)
item.description:
The Fund for UFO Research (FUFOR) was a non-profit UFO research group founded in 1979 in Mount Rainier, Maryland. Its stated goals were to advance scholarly research into UFOs and the extraterrestrial hypothesis, and to facilitate the release of classified U.S. government documents related to these topics. From 1979 to 2006, FUFOR provided over $700,000 in research grants and supported investigations into subjects like the MJ-12 papers and Project Blue Book. The organization has not had an active online or print presence since 2011.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fund_for_UFO_Research
